executive_summary: Oracle has released an emergency fix for a critical pre-authentication remote code execution flaw (CVE-2026-21992) in Identity Manager that is easily exploitable and rated maximum severity. CISA has ordered federal agencies to patch a maximum severity Cisco flaw (CVE-2026-20131) and has added six Apple vulnerabilities linked to the DarkSword iOS exploit chain to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog, requiring patches by April 3, 2026. Multiple major data breaches were reported: Crunchyroll (100GB user data), Ameriprise Financial (by ShinyHunters gang), and AstraZeneca. Russian and Iranian state-linked hackers are conducting active phishing campaigns targeting Signal, WhatsApp, and Telegram users, with the FBI and CISA issuing joint warnings.

canada_landscape.summary: Canada-specific incidents include a copyright-themed PureLog Stealer credential theft campaign explicitly targeting Canadian users via phishing. Dark web posts claim alleged data breaches affecting Rogers (Canadian telco) and potential U.S. Tax Office data sales. No CCCS advisories were identified in this 48-hour period. Insider threat concerns are rising in Canadian organizations according to industry reports.
canada_landscape.financial: Ameriprise Financial (U.S.-based but serves Canadian clients) breached by ShinyHunters gang

report_2w.executive_summary: Cisco Secure Firewall Management Center is under active ransomware attack via a critical zero-day flaw (CVE-2026-20131) that the Interlock group exploited 36 days before Cisco disclosed it. CISA has ordered federal agencies to patch by Sunday and added five new exploited vulnerabilities to its catalog, including flaws in Apple iOS, Microsoft SharePoint, and Zimbra Collaboration Suite. A massive DoJ operation disrupted three IoT botnets responsible for record-breaking 31.4 Tbps DDoS attacks that hit targets in Canada. The U.S. confirmed Iran's government operates the Handala hacktivist group and seized their domains, though new ones appeared within hours.

items.name: Interlock Ransomware Group
items.targets: Organizations using Cisco Secure Firewall Management Center
items.ttps: T1190 (Exploit Public-Facing Application), T1068 (Exploitation for Privilege Escalation), T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact). Exploited CVE-2026-20131 zero-day 36 days before vendor disclosure to gain root access.

items.name: Handala (Iran Government-Linked)
items.targets: U.S. and Israeli organizations including Stryker Corporation
items.ttps: T1498 (Network Denial of Service), T1589 (Gather Victim Identity Information), T1566 (Phishing). U.S. confirmed group operates under Iranian government direction. Launched new domains within hours of FBI seizure.

items.name: Lazarus Group (North Korea)
items.ttps: T1566 (Phishing), T1078 (Valid Accounts), T1657 (Financial Theft). State-sponsored financial warfare operations targeting crypto assets.

items.name: AISURU, Kimwolf, JackSkid Botnet Operators
items.targets: 3 million IoT devices globally, including targets in Canada
items.ttps: T1498.001 (Direct Network Flood), T1584.005 (Botnet). Responsible for record 31.4 Tbps DDoS attacks. Disrupted by DoJ operation.

items.name: Russian APT (Unspecified)
items.ttps: T1190 (Exploit Public-Facing Application), T1078 (Valid Accounts). Weaponized CVE-2025-66376 Zimbra vulnerability in Ukraine-targeted intrusions.

items.name: The Gentlemen Ransomware Gang
items.targets: Various organizations, inner workings recently leaked
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1490 (Inhibit System Recovery), T1562.001 (Disable or Modify Tools). Associated with Qilin ransomware infrastructure.

items.name: Akira Ransomware
items.ttps: T1562.001 (Disable or Modify Tools), T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact). Expanded EDR killer tactics using BYOVD (Bring Your Own Vulnerable Driver) technique exploiting 34 signed vulnerable drivers.

items.name: Qilin Ransomware
items.ttps: T1562.001 (Disable or Modify Tools), T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact). Uses BYOVD technique to disable security solutions.

items.name: LockBit Ransomware
items.ttps: T1562.001 (Disable or Modify Tools), T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact). Expanded EDR killer tactics beyond vulnerable drivers.

items.name: ShinyHunters
items.targets: Aura data breach exposing 900,000 contact records
items.ttps: T1530 (Data from Cloud Storage), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service). Exposed customer contact information from Aura breach.

items.name: Perseus Android Malware
items.ttps: T1417 (Input Capture), T1430 (Location Tracking), T1533 (Data from Local System). Steals user notes and enables full device takeover.

items.name: Horabot Banking Trojan
items.ttps: T1566.001 (Spearphishing Attachment), T1106 (Native API), T1071.001 (Web Protocols). Resurfaces with multi-stage phishing and email worm tactics.

items.name: Russian Intelligence Services (FSB/GRU)
items.targets: Signal messaging app users including journalists and activists
items.ttps: T1566.002 (Spearphishing Link), T1056.002 (GUI Input Capture), T1539 (Steal Web Session Cookie). FBI linked Signal phishing attacks to Russian intelligence.

canada_landscape.summary: Canada was directly impacted by the massive IoT botnet DDoS attacks that reached record 31.4 Tbps levels, prompting a successful DoJ disruption operation. Canadian organizations are urged to prioritize patching of the five new CISA KEV vulnerabilities. No CCCS advisories were identified in this reporting period. A broader analysis highlights the need for strengthened cybersecurity in Canada's municipal sector.
